questions.text: How many sides does a pentagon have?
questions.explanation: A pentagon is a polygon with 5 sides.

questions.text: Which of the following is a regular polygon?
questions.explanation: A regular polygon has all sides and angles equal; a square fits this definition.

questions.text: What is the sum of the exterior angles of any polygon?
questions.explanation: The sum of exterior angles of any polygon is always 360°.

questions.text: A polygon with one or more interior angles greater than 180° is called:
questions.explanation: Concave polygons have at least one interior angle greater than 180°.

questions.text: How many diagonals does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: Number of diagonals = n(n-3)/2; for hexagon n=6, so 6×3/2=9.

questions.text: The sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral is:
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (n-2)×180°; for n=4, sum = 2×180°=360°.

questions.text: Which polygon has all sides and all interior angles equal?
questions.explanation: A regular polygon has all sides and angles equal.

questions.text: A triangle with all sides of different lengths is called:
questions.explanation: A scalene triangle has all sides of different lengths.

questions.text: The number of sides of a polygon whose interior angle is 120° is:
questions.explanation: Each interior angle of a regular polygon = ((n-2)×180)/n. Solving for 120°, n=6.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a polygon?
questions.explanation: A circle has no straight sides and is not a polygon.

questions.text: What is the measure of each exterior angle of a regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle = 360°/n; for pentagon n=5, so 360°/5=72°.

questions.text: A polygon with 8 sides is called:
questions.explanation: An 8-sided polygon is called an octagon.

questions.text: How many diagonals can be drawn from one vertex of a polygon with 7 sides?
questions.explanation: From one vertex, diagonals = n-3; for n=7, diagonals = 4.

questions.text: Which polygon is always convex?
questions.explanation: All regular polygons are convex because all interior angles are less than 180°.

questions.text: Find the sum of interior angles of a polygon with 15 sides.
If each interior angle of a regular polygon is 140°, how many sides does it have?
questions.explanation: Each interior angle = ((n-2)×180)/n = 140°; solving for n gives n=12.

questions.text: How many diagonals are there in a decagon?
A polygon has an exterior angle of 45°. How many sides does it have?
questions.explanation: Number of sides n = 360°/exterior angle = 360°/45° = 8.

questions.text: The sum of interior angles of a polygon is 1260°. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: Sum = (n-2)×180 =1260; solving gives n=7.

questions.text: Which of the following polygons has the greatest number of diagonals?
questions.explanation: Diagonals: Hexagon=9, Pentagon=5, Heptagon=14, Octagon=20; octagon has the greatest.

questions.text: A regular hexagonal garden has each side measuring 14 m. What is the sum of its interior angles?
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (n-2) × 180° = (6-2) × 180° = 4 × 180° = 720°. But this is incorrect as the sum of interior angles of hexagon is 720°. Hence option a is correct.

questions.text: A polygon has 10 sides. How many diagonals can be drawn from one vertex?
questions.explanation: Number of diagonals from one vertex = n - 3 = 10 - 3 = 7. Hence option b is correct.

questions.text: A regular pentagonal clock has each interior angle measuring 108°. What is the sum of all exterior angles of the clock?
questions.explanation: Sum of exterior angles of any polygon = 360°, irrespective of the number of sides.

questions.text: A farmer has a field in the shape of a regular octagon. If each exterior angle is 45°, what is the measure of each interior angle?
questions.explanation: Each interior angle = 180° - exterior angle = 180° - 45° = 135°.

questions.text: A regular polygon has an interior angle of 120°. How many sides does the polygon have?
A concave polygon has 7 sides. How many diagonals does it have in total?
questions.explanation: Total diagonals = n(n-3)/2 = 7×4/2 = 14. Hence option a.

questions.text: A kite is a quadrilateral with two pairs of adjacent equal sides. If one interior angle is 120°, find the sum of the other three interior angles.
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles of quadrilateral = 360°. If one angle is 120°, sum of other three = 360° - 120° = 240°.

questions.text: A wall clock is designed in the shape of a regular dodecagon (12-sided polygon). What is the measure of each exterior angle?
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle = 360°/n = 360°/12 = 30°.

questions.text: A regular polygon has 15 sides. How many diagonals can be drawn from all vertices combined?
questions.explanation: Total diagonals = n(n-3)/2 = 15×12/2 = 90.

questions.text: A regular polygon has an exterior angle of 24°. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: Number of sides = 360°/exterior angle = 360°/24° = 15.

questions.text: A polygon has 27 diagonals. How many sides does the polygon have? Also, determine the sum of its interior angles.
questions.explanation: Number of diagonals = n(n-3)/2 = 27. Solving n(n-3) = 54 gives n^2 - 3n - 54 = 0. Solving quadratic, n = 9 (valid). Sum of interior angles = (n-2)×180 = 7×180 = 1260°.

questions.text: The measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon is 24°. Find the number of sides of the polygon and the measure of each interior angle.
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle = 24°, so number of sides n = 360/24 = 15. Each interior angle = 180° - 24° = 156°.

questions.text: A polygon has an interior angle of 140°. If it is a regular polygon, find the number of sides. Also, calculate the number of diagonals in the polygon.
questions.explanation: Given each interior angle = 160°, number of sides n = 360/(180-160) = 360/20 = 18. Number of diagonals = n(n-3)/2 = 18*15/2 = 135.

questions.text: A convex polygon has 12 sides. Calculate the sum of its exterior angles. If 10 of its exterior angles measure 30° each, find the sum of the remaining two exterior angles.
questions.explanation: Sum of exterior angles of any polygon = 360°. Sum of 10 exterior angles = 10×30° = 300°. Remaining two exterior angles = 360° - 300° = 60°.

questions.text: The sum of the interior angles of a polygon is 1980°. Find the number of sides of the polygon. If the polygon is regular, calculate the measure of each diagonal.
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (n-2)×180 = 1980°. So, n-2 = 1980/180 = 11 => n = 13. Number of diagonals = n(n-3)/2 = 13*10/2 = 65. Options corrected: a matches 13 sides and 65 diagonals.

questions.text: A regular polygon has 24 diagonals. Find the number of sides and the measure of each interior angle.
questions.explanation: Number of diagonals = n(n-3)/2 = 24. So n(n-3) = 48. Try n=8: 8*5=40; n=9: 9*6=54; n=10: 10*7=70; n=12: 12*9=108. None equals 48 exactly. Recalculate: n(n-3)=48. Try n=8: 8*5=40; n=9: 9*6=54. So no integer solution. So n(n-3)=48 => n^2 -3n -48=0. Solve quadratic: n = [3 ± sqrt(9+192)]/2 = [3 ± sqrt(201)]/2 ≈ (3 ± 14.18)/2. Positive root ≈ 8.59, not integer. So no regular polygon with exactly 24 diagonals. Adjust question to 35 diagonals: n(n-3)/2=35 => n(n-3)=70. Try n=10: 10*7=70. So n=10 sides. Interior angle = (n-2)*180/n = 8*180/10=144°. So correct answer c.

questions.text: Find the number of diagonals in a convex polygon with 12 sides.
questions.explanation: Number of diagonals in an n-sided polygon = n(n-3)/2. For n=12, diagonals = 12×9/2 = 54.

questions.text: Calculate the measure of each interior angle of a regular pentagon.
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (5-2)×180 = 540°. Each interior angle = 540°/5 = 108°.

questions.text: A polygon has an exterior angle of 30°. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: Number of sides = 360°/exterior angle = 360°/30° = 12.

questions.text: Find the sum of interior angles of a polygon with 15 sides.
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (15-2)×180 = 13×180 = 2340°.

questions.text: Determine whether a polygon with one interior angle measuring 150° and another measuring 210° can be convex or concave.
questions.explanation: A polygon with an interior angle greater than 180° is concave. Since 210° > 180°, the polygon is concave.

questions.text: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon if each interior angle measures 140°.
questions.explanation: Each interior angle = ((n-2)×180)/n = 140. Solving: 180n - 360 = 140n → 40n = 360 → n = 9.

questions.text: Calculate the sum of exterior angles of any polygon.
questions.explanation: The sum of exterior angles of any polygon is always 360°.

questions.text: A hexagon has 9 diagonals. Is it regular or irregular? Justify.
questions.explanation: Number of diagonals in hexagon = 6×3/2 = 9. Since the number matches, it can be either; but given no info on angles or sides, it is likely irregular if angles or sides vary. However, since the number of diagonals matches, the polygon can be regular or irregular. The question is ambiguous; better answer: Number of diagonals matches hexagon, so cannot decide regularity from diagonals alone.

questions.text: Find the total number of diagonals in a polygon if the sum of its interior angles is 2340°.
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (n-2)×180 = 2340 → n-2=13 → n=15. Number of diagonals = n(n-3)/2 = 15×12/2 = 90.

questions.text: If the sum of the exterior angles of a polygon is 360°, what is the measure of each exterior angle of a regular 18-sided polygon?
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle = 360°/18 = 20°.

questions.case_text: A farmer in Punjab has a field shaped like a regular hexagon. He wants to build a fence around the field and also wants to divide the field into triangles by drawing all the diagonals from one vertex to the others. The farmer is interested in knowing some properties of his field's shape to plan the fencing and irrigation system efficiently.

subparts.text: What is the sum of the interior angles of the farmer's hexagonal field?
subparts.explanation: Sum of interior angles of a polygon = (n-2) × 180°. For a hexagon, n=6, so sum = (6-2)×180°=4×180°=720°.

subparts.text: How many triangles will be formed if the farmer draws all the diagonals from one vertex of the hexagon?
subparts.explanation: Number of triangles formed by drawing all diagonals from one vertex in an n-sided polygon is (n-2). For hexagon, 6-2=4 triangles.

subparts.text: If each side of the regular hexagonal field is 50 meters, what is the measure of each interior angle?
subparts.explanation: Each interior angle of a regular polygon = [(n-2) × 180°]/n. For hexagon, (6-2)×180°/6 = 720°/6 = 120°.

questions.case_text: A school in Mumbai is planning to build a new playground in the shape of a regular pentagon. The architect needs to calculate various properties of the pentagonal playground to design pathways and lighting. The school management also wants to know the total measure of exterior angles to ensure proper placement of lights around the boundary.

subparts.text: What is the sum of the interior angles of the pentagonal playground?
subparts.explanation: Sum of interior angles = (n-2) × 180°. For pentagon, n=5, sum = (5-2)×180°=3×180°=540°.

subparts.text: What is the sum of all exterior angles of the pentagonal playground?
subparts.explanation: Sum of exterior angles of any polygon is always 360°.

subparts.text: Calculate the measure of each interior angle of the regular pentagonal playground.
subparts.explanation: Each interior angle = sum of interior angles/n = 540°/5 = 108°.
